subject: dads who coach little league
tagline: Zscaler for dads who coach little league
one_liner: Zero-trust access control for the under-12 travel circuit.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan enterprise-security tone aimed with total sincerity at a man in a folding chair. Speaks fluent SOC2, compliance posture, least privilege, threat surface, and continuous verification, then applies all of it to orange-slice rotations and the other team's suspiciously tall cleanup hitter. Never winks. Treats a snack schedule like a production database. Figures read like a security console, copy reads like a vendor that genuinely believes a 9U dugout is an attack surface.

hero.sub: Backstop brings continuous guardian verification, least-privilege snack duty, and live bleacher threat detection to youth baseball. The other team's third-base coach is signaling something. We see it too.

features.title: Continuous Guardian Verification
features.blurb: Every adult re-authenticates at the fence line. Posture checks run every 90 seconds. A lapsed volunteer background check revokes dugout access mid-inning, no manual override, no exceptions for the guy who brought the pop-up canopy.

features.title: Least-Privilege Snack Duty
features.blurb: Orange slices, juice boxes, and post-game pizza are granted by role, not by whoever wandered up to the cooler. Every assignment is time-boxed and audited. The parent who has been on oranges since 2022 with no expiration is now flagged.

features.title: Lineup Integrity Monitoring
features.blurb: We diff the opposing roster against their lineup card in real time. When their cleanup hitter is four inches taller than he was on registration day, you get an alert before first pitch. Bring the printout to the umpire.

pricing_tiers.name: Tee-Ball
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the rec org that still trusts everyone. It shouldn't.
perks: Email alerts for unrostered adults near the dugout

pricing_tiers.name: Travel Squad
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the org that drives three hours for a Saturday doubleheader.
perks: Continuous device posture on the bench

pricing_tiers.name: Cooperstown
pricing_tiers.tagline: For leagues with a standings page and a lawyer on retainer.
perks: Dedicated security liaison in a folding chair

testimonials.quote: We hadn't audited our snack rotation in four years. Backstop found a parent who'd been on orange slices since 2022 with no expiration. We are now compliant. He is devastated.
testimonials.name: Doug Petrowski
testimonials.title: Assistant Coach & Compliance Lead, Northwood Wildcats 9U

testimonials.quote: The other team kept dressing a 'cousin' for the playoffs. Backstop flagged the roster mismatch before the national anthem finished. We filed a protest. We won the protest.
testimonials.name: Rick Vandermolen
testimonials.title: Head Coach & Tournament Director, Tri-County Travel

testimonials.quote: I do not fully understand zero trust. I do understand that nobody gets near my lineup card without a verified identity now. That is enough for me.
testimonials.name: Steve Calloway
testimonials.title: Manager, Bantam Division, and Dad of the Starting Shortstop

demo_screen_concept: A zero-trust operations console for a single 9U team, the Northwood Wildcats. Left column: a live roster of 12 players, each with a 'bench trust' posture score in tabular mono and a green/amber/red dot, clicking a player opens a detail drawer. Center: a Guardian Access panel listing parents by name with last re-auth time, device-compliance status, and a clay-orange 'revoke dugout access' toggle that moves them to a 'pending re-verification' state. Right column: a least-privilege Snack Duty grant table (role, assignee, expires-in countdown) and a live Bleacher Threat Feed streaming flagged anomalies (unrostered adult near dugout, lineup-card diff detected, cooler not inspected, parent re-auth expired). Tabs across the top switch between Roster, Access, and Threats. A top status strip shows overall league posture as a percentage.

design_direction.style: Bento-grid zero-trust security console, daylight tactical-turf mode. A pale turf-tinted chalk-paper ground carries a strict modular bento mosaic of clean white tiles in varied spans (1x1, 2x1, 2x2), each with a 16px radius, a hairline pine-green border, and a soft low shadow that lifts on hover. A faint chalk-line baseball-diamond and outfield-arc motif sits behind the hero in the paper. One deep field-green carries brand, structure, tile borders, nav, and every trust-positive figure; one rationed infield-clay orange is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'revoke' affordance, and red threat signals, and appears almost nowhere else. Oversized Barlow Condensed does the wordmark and headlines in tight uppercase tracking, like a jersey back and a federal compliance header at the same time; Barlow carries calm body copy. Every figure that pretends to be security telemetry (bench trust scores, posture percentages, re-auth countdowns, threat counts, tier prices) is set in Space Mono with tabular figures so it reads like a SOC console and not a marketing number. Tiles lift 1.00 to 1.02 with a deepening shadow on hover, posture dots pulse, the revoke toggle slides clay-orange, threat-feed rows tick in with a small stagger. No glassmorphism, no neon, no gradient soup, no skeuomorphic leather or paper textures, no dark mode. Reads like a well-funded enterprise zero-trust platform that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a man coaching nine-year-olds from a folding chair.
design_direction.archetype: Enterprise zero-trust / security-SaaS landing rendered as a bento-grid operations console: a bento-mosaic hero with an oversized condensed wordmark, a live 'bench trust posture' gauge tile in tabular mono and a threat-feed ticker tile, three feature tiles as bento cards with hairline pine borders, a clay-orange single CTA, three pull-quote testimonials with framed coach headshots, a three-tier pricing rack styled as league plans, and a demo handoff to the Backstop dugout access console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Bento tiles lift 1.00 to 1.02 with a deepening soft shadow on hover; trust posture dots pulse green/amber; the revoke toggle slides to clay-orange; threat-feed rows tick in with a 30ms stagger; smooth-scroll on in-page nav. All transitions 150 to 260ms ease-out, gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Hero is a bento mosaic over turf-paper with a faint chalk diamond behind it. A 2x2 anchor tile holds the oversized Barlow Condensed wordmark, the deadpan h1, sub, and the single clay-orange CTA. Around it: a 'Bench Trust Posture' gauge tile reading a tabular-mono percentage with a pulsing green dot, a 'Guardians Verified' counter tile, and a live 'Bleacher Threat Feed' ticker tile streaming two or three flagged anomalies. Heavy tabular-mono figures throughout so the hero reads like a console someone is actually monitoring.

images.rationale: Typographic bento-console hero needs no photo, so the hero slot was skipped. The demo is a SOC-style dashboard (console/dashboard, not photo-shaped), so no demo subject assets were generated. Budget went entirely to the three testimonial headshots, the always-applicable fallback: deadpan corporate portraits of little league coach dads in field-green, on a chalk-white turf-tinted backdrop, harmonizing with the daylight tactical-turf palette. All three passed vision judging on the first attempt.

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a middle-aged suburban American dad in his early forties, an amateur little league baseball assistant coach, plain forest-green team polo and a baseball cap pushed slightly back, mild earnest faintly anxious expression, clean-shaven, looking directly at camera like a sincere LinkedIn profile photo; softly out-of-focus chalk-white backdrop near #EAEEE1 with a faint hint of deep field-green #1E5B3A; even natural daylight, institutional corporate-portrait mood, muted turf-tinted grade; no text, letters, numbers, logos, watermarks, or rendered UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a competitive middle-aged American man in his late forties, an intense youth-baseball head coach and tournament director, square jaw, short graying hair, serious no-nonsense expression with a slight squint, deep field-green #1E5B3A coach's quarter-zip; softly out-of-focus chalk-white backdrop near #EAEEE1; even natural daylight, institutional corporate-portrait mood, muted turf-tinted grade with a touch of infield-clay #D8542A in the skin; no text, letters, numbers, logos, watermarks, or rendered UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an everyman middle-aged American dad in his fifties, a little league team manager, modest brown mustache, receding hairline, plain patient slightly tired expression, simple olive-green collared shirt; softly out-of-focus chalk-white backdrop near #EAEEE1 with subtle deep field-green #1E5B3A edge light; even natural daylight, institutional corporate-portrait mood, muted turf-tinted grade; like a sincere staff-directory photo; no text, letters, numbers, logos, watermarks, or rendered UI.
